directions_tried.direction: Phase 0 definition gate for a physical-space delayed-transfer event
directions_tried.notes: Exploration 001 passed. The preferred event is an exact-NS localized forward-flux burst across one coarse-graining scale interface, with abrupt gain in a narrow subscale witness channel. The Duchon-Robert burst survives only as backup.

directions_tried.direction: Phase 1 precursor-and-fidelity gate for the exact event E_flux
directions_tried.notes: Exploration 002 passed. The preferred precursor is earlier cumulative positive forward flux on the predecessor cylinder for the same scale interface; Duchon-Robert survives only as backup.

directions_tried.direction: Phase 2 quantitative test for the pair (E_flux, P_flux^-)
directions_tried.notes: Exploration 003 showed the exact filtered-energy balance is too time-local to force any no-hidden-transfer statement from small earlier P_flux^- to exclusion of later E_flux. The strongest pair fails by structural lack of backward memory.

explorations_completed.direction: Phase 0 definition gate for a physical-space delayed-transfer event
explorations_completed.key_takeaway: A physical-space delayed-transfer event can be stated on exact NS itself: delayed localized forward interscale flux plus abrupt local band-energy gain. The mission no longer needs a reduced packet/circuit object at Phase 0.

explorations_completed.direction: Phase 1 precursor-and-fidelity gate for the exact event E_flux
explorations_completed.key_takeaway: The only precursor pair that survives is earlier cumulative positive forward flux on the fixed predecessor cylinder for the same scale interface. This keeps the mission on exact NS and sets up a direct no-hidden-transfer test.

explorations_completed.direction: Phase 2 quantitative test for the pair (E_flux, P_flux^-)
explorations_completed.key_takeaway: The exact filtered-energy identities do not control the earlier precursor slab R_- from the later event window. The preferred exact pair therefore fails as a hidden-precursor firewall object by structural time-locality.
